ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 301, NO APPEARANCES, NOT CONTESTED, AND THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF IS GRANTED UNDER C.C.P. SECTION 36(A). THE TRIAL DATE IS FEBRUARY 10, 2025, AT 11:15 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 206. LAST DAY PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 36(F) IS FEBRUARY 13, 2025. THE LAST DAY FOR HEARING SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION MOTIONS IS FEBRUARY 4, 2025. A FURTHER CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE IS SET FOR DECEMBER 17, 2024, AT 1:30 P.M. A MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE IS SET FOR THURSDAY, FEBRUARY 6, 2025, AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 503. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ROCHELLE C. EAST. CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA. NOT REPORTED. BAILIFF: NOT PRESENT. (301/RCE)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE - TRIAL COURTS
Off Calendar Dec-11-2024 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Oct-15-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, not contested, and the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Plaintiff's Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under C.C.P. section 36(a).

1. The trial date is February 10, 2025, at 11:15 a.m. in Department 206.
a. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is February 13, 2025.
b. The parties shall follow the California Rules of Court, San Francisco Local Rules, and Local Rule 20.
2. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is February 4, 2025.
a. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise.
b. Before a party files and serves a summary judgment/adjudication motion, it must contact the clerk to make a reservation.
c. The Court allows a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared, unless good cause is found to exceed this number. Contact the clerk to schedule a good cause hearing.
3. Time to respond to written discovery not yet served is shortened to 20 days.
a. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier.
b. Any issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, shall occur in person or via telephone, not by email or letter.
4. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service.
5. The fact discovery cut-off date is January 24, 2025.
6. The expert discovery cut-off date is February 7, 2025.
7. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than November 14, 2024.
a. If Plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify Defendants no later than five days after submission.
b. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
8. No later than October 29, 2024:
a. Plaintiff must provide a statement to each Defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting Plaintiff's claims as to each Defendant.
b. Defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of facts supporting Defendant's affirmative defenses.
c. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary judgment motion. (Mini minutes continue in Part 2 of 2.)

PLAINTIFF EDWARD SCHULZE, MARILYN SCHULZE NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF (ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR TENTATIVE RULING ENTRY PURPOSES ONLY.)
Off Calendar Oct-15-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
(Part 2 of 2)
(Mini minutes continue from previous entry.)

9. A further case management conference is set for December 17, 2024, at 1:30 p.m.
a. Case management conference statements are not required.
b. Parties may appear at the conference by zoom.
c. If the parties agree that no CMC is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two court days before the hearing.

10. Plaintiffs will send a settlement demand to Defendants no later than December 30, 2024. Response to the settlement demand is due January 13, 2024. The parties shall separately submit confidential settlement demands/offers. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Department503@sftc.org via electronic mail on January 20, 2025.

11. A mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, February 6, 2025, at 9:30 a.m. in Department 503. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is excused for good cause. A request to excuse a personal appearance must be submitted to Department503@sftc.org two weeks before the mandatory settlement conference.

Order signed.

Judge: Rochelle C. East. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Not Reported. Bailiff: Not Present. (301/RCE)
